label: zal | desire | ∅
sentence: Soon Zal desired to explore the kingdom.

label: he | set | escort
sentence: He set out with a grand escort and, after some travel, proudly entered Kabul.

label: mihrab | be | ∅
sentence: Mihrab, the ruler there and a descendant of the serpent Zohak, was wise and noble.

label: mihrab | go | ∅
sentence: Hearing that Zal, son of Sam and to whom he paid tribute, was approaching, Mihrab went out to greet him with his nobles and slaves bearing valuable gifts.

label: zal | prepare | feast
sentence: Zal, upon learning of Mihrab’s arrival, prepared a feast in his tents.

label: mihrab | din | he
sentence: Mihrab and his retinue dined with him late into the night.

label: zal | praise | beauty
sentence: After the guests left, Zal praised Mihrab’s beauty.

label: nobleman | stand | ∅
sentence: Then a nobleman stood and told him, “O Zal, you know not true beauty since you have not seen this man’s daughter.

label: face | shin | ∅
sentence: She is like a slender cypress, her face shines brighter than the sun, and her mouth is like a pomegranate blossom.”

label: heart | fill | longing
sentence: Hearing this, Zal’s heart was filled with longing, and he could not sleep, thinking of her beauty.

label: he | open | door
sentence: At dawn, he opened his court doors, and the nobles gathered according to rank.

label: mihrab | arrive | ∅
sentence: Mihrab arrived to greet him from outside the city gates.

label: zal | want | ∅
sentence: Zal wanted Mihrab to ask a favor of him.

label: mihrab | speak | ∅
sentence: Mihrab spoke: “O mighty ruler, I ask only that you stay as a guest under my roof so my heart may rejoice in your presence.”

label: zal | answer | ∅
sentence: Zal answered, “O King, do not ask this favor; it cannot be done.

label: shah | be | ∅
sentence: The Shah and Sam would be angry if they knew I dined under the roof of Zohak’s kin.

label: ∅ | ask | anything
sentence: Ask for anything else.”

label: mihrab | leave | ∅
sentence: Mihrab left sadly, bowing before Zal.

label: zal | watch | ∅
sentence: Zal watched him go and once more praised him.

label: day | pass | ∅
sentence: Then his thoughts returned to the King’s daughter and her beauty; days passed unnoticed as desire consumed him.

label: mihrab | visit | quarter
sentence: One morning Mihrab visited the women’s quarters, where he found Sindokht, his wife, and their daughter Rodabeh.

label: house | be | garden
sentence: The house was like a garden full of color and sweet scents, and the two women shone like moons of beauty.

label: mihrab | marvel | loveliness
sentence: After greeting Rodabeh, Mihrab marveled at her loveliness and blessed her.

label: sindokht | ask | mihrab
sentence: Sindokht asked Mihrab about the stranger in their city’s tents: “Tell me, what kind of man is this white-haired son of Sam?

label: mihrab | reply | ∅
sentence:
Is he worthy of our nest, or even the throne?”

Mihrab replied, “O fair cypress, the son of Sam is a hero among men.

label: heart | be | lion
sentence: His heart is like a lion’s, his strength an elephant’s.

label: he | be | crocodile
sentence: He is graceful to friends, a fierce crocodile to foes.

label: lock | add | glory
sentence: Even his white locks add to his glory, turning flaws into beauty.”

label: heart | burn | love
sentence: Hearing this, Rodabeh’s heart burned with love for Zal.

label: she | be | ∅
sentence: She could neither eat nor rest; she was like one transformed.

label: she | confide | slave
sentence: Unable to bear it alone, she confided in her beloved slaves, begging them to keep her secret and help ease her troubled heart.

label: slave | beg | she
sentence: Fearful, the slaves begged her to let go of a man marked by men and rejected by his own father, but Rodabeh would not listen.

label: wise | speak | ∅
sentence: Seeing her resolve, the wisest among them spoke: “O moon-faced beauty and slender cypress, it shall be done as you wish.

label: we | rest | ∅
sentence: We will not rest until this royal youth lies at your feet.”

label: rodabeh | be | ∅
sentence: Rodabeh was glad and promised to plant a noble tree for the faithful slave, which would bear riches, jewels, and wisdom.

label: slave | go | work
sentence: The slaves went to work, knowing their plan must be subtle.

label: they | enter | garden
sentence: Dressed in fine clothes, they cheerfully entered the garden by the river outside the city, picking roses and scattering blossoms in the water to tell fortunes.

label: they | come | tent
sentence: There they came near Zal’s tents.

label: one | say | ∅
sentence:
Zal noticed them and asked who they were; one said, “They are slaves sent by the moon of Kabul into the garden of flowers.”

Zal’s heart leapt with joy.

label: he | go | river
sentence: He went to the river with one page.

label: he | shoot | ∅
sentence: Seeing a waterbird flying up, he shot and struck it through the heart, and it fell near the rose-gatherers.

label: he | send | boy
sentence: He sent the boy to retrieve it.

label: he | be | king
sentence: Surely he is king among men.”

label: boy | answer | ∅
sentence: The boy answered, “Don’t you know the son of Sam, the hero?

label: ∅ | be | equal
sentence: There is no equal in strength or beauty.”

label: woman | object | ∅
sentence: The women objected, “Nay, boast not so.

label: house | hold | sun
sentence: The house of Mihrab holds a sun that outshines all else.”

label: boy | smile | ∅
sentence: The boy smiled and returned to Zal.

label: zal | ask | ∅
sentence: Zal asked why he smiled so, and he told him what the women said.

label: zal | send | he
sentence: Zal sent him back with gifts of pearl and gold, asking them to stay and carry jewels with their roses.

label: slave | ask | ∅
sentence: The slave who had sworn to serve Rodabeh asked to see Zal’s face, saying, “A secret known by three is no secret.”

label: zal | allow | it
sentence: Zal allowed it, and she told him of Rodabeh’s beauty.

label: passion | grow | ∅
sentence: His passion grew, and he asked her to show the way to see the maiden.

label: slave | say | ∅
sentence: The slave said, “Let us return to the women’s house.

label: we | praise | son
sentence: We will praise the son of Sam to Rodabeh and weave the net that will trap the lion in his chase of the lamb.”

label: zal | agree | ∅
sentence: Zal agreed, and the women returned, joyful, saying, “The lion is entering the snare, and the wishes of Rodabeh and Zal will come to pass.”

label: porter | scold | they
sentence: Yet, at the city gates, a porter scolded them for leaving while the stranger stayed in Kabul.

label: they | calm | anger
sentence: They calmed his anger and reached Rodabeh.

label: they | tell | she
sentence: They told her of Zal’s beauty and prowess.

label: she | smile | ∅
sentence: She smiled and said, “Why have you changed your tune?

label: you | mock | youth
sentence: You once mocked this bird-reared youth with the sage’s locks, but now sing his praises with loud voices.”

label: rodabeh | prepare | home
sentence: Rodabeh secretly prepared her home to honor a guest, adorning it with brocades from Rome and silks from India, perfumed with musk and ambergris, and blossoming with flowers.

label: all | be | ∅
sentence: As night fell, a slave came to Zal, whispering, “Come, all is ready.”

label: he | follow | she
sentence: He followed her.

label: zal | see | house
sentence: At the women’s house, Zal saw Rodabeh standing on the roof.

label: beauty | be | cypress
sentence: Her beauty was like a cypress under a full moon.

label: she | welcome | he
sentence: She welcomed him: “O young man, son of a hero, Heaven’s blessings upon you!”

label: zal | reply | prayer
sentence: Zal replied with prayers to meet her more closely, for she was above on the roof, and he below.

label: rodabeh | let | hair
sentence: Rodabeh let down her long hair, saying, “Here is a flawless rope.

label: climb | ensnare | you
sentence: Climb, O warrior, and seize my dark locks; it is fitting I ensnare you.”

label: i | become | you
sentence: Zal replied, “No, fair one; I would ill become you if I harmed you.”

label: he | kiss | hair
sentence: He kissed her hair, called for a rope, tied a running knot, threw it up, and climbed to the roof.

label: they | enter | chamber
sentence: Hand in hand, they entered the golden chambers, surrounded by slaves.

label: they | gaze | one
sentence: They gazed upon one another, recognizing great beauty, and hours passed with sweet conversation as love grew.

label: zal | say | ∅
sentence: Zal said, “O cypress, scented with musk, when Minuchihr and Sam learn this, they will be angry, accusing me of forgetting God and lifting their hands against me.

label: i | swear | ∅
sentence: But I swear this life is worthless if not spent with you.

label: i | call | heaven
sentence: I call upon Heaven to hear me: none

label: you | be | bride
sentence: but you shall be my bride.”

label: rodabeh | swear | oath
sentence: Rodabeh swore the same oath.

label: drum | sound | tent
sentence: Dawn’s drums sounded from the King’s tents, announcing the day’s arrival.

label: zal | cry | glory
sentence: Zal and Rodabeh cried together, “O glory of the world, stay a while, do not come so quickly!”

label: sun | pay | heed
sentence: But the sun paid no heed, and it was time to part.

label: zal | let | ∅
sentence: Zal let himself down from the battlements and left his beloved’s house.

label: zal | receive | greeting
sentence: When daylight filled the earth, Zal received the usual greetings from nobles and chiefs.

label: he | gather | mubid
sentence: He gathered his Mubids (wise men) and told them of his love for a daughter of the serpent lineage.

label: none | wish | ∅
sentence: The Mubids were troubled and kept silent; none wished to poison the sweetness of this love.

label: zal | urge | they
sentence: Zal urged them to speak, promising rich gifts.

label: they | say | ∅
sentence: They said a king’s honor is not hurt by a woman, and though Mihrab was descended from Zohak, he was noble and brave.

label: they | advise | zal
sentence: They advised Zal to write to his father and ask Sam to appear before the Shah.

label: zal | call | scribe
sentence: Zal called a scribe and dictated his letter.

label: he | tell | sam
sentence: He told Sam of his love and fears, reminded him of being cast out at birth, raised by a bird in a nest beneath the sun, while his father lived in silk.

label: ∅ | recall | promise
sentence: He did not defend what happened, only recalled Sam’s promise.

label: he | give | letter
sentence: He gave the letter to a messenger to ride swiftly to Sam.

label: sam | trouble | ∅
sentence: Sam was troubled when he heard.

label: woe | be | i
sentence: “Woe is me.

label: fosterling | seek | ∅
sentence: The wild bird’s fosterling seeks to unite with a cursed race.”

label: what | come | union
sentence: He pondered his reply: if he forbade Zal’s desire, he’d break his oath; if he allowed it, what could come from the union of a Deev and a bird’s son?

label: he | call | mubid
sentence: Burdened, he called his Mubids to read the stars, for blending fire and water could bring disaster.

label: they | cast | horoscope
sentence: All day they cast the horoscope for Zal and Rodabeh.

label: they | return | dusk
sentence: At dusk, they returned rejoicing.

label: spring | bring | son
sentence: To Sam they said, “Hail, Sam, for we have read the skies; A clear spring will bring a son to Zal, a hero unmatched in power and glory in Iran.”

label: heart | lift | ∅
sentence: Sam’s heart lifted.

label: he | reward | man
sentence: He rewarded the wise men and sent the messenger back to Zal with words: “I find your passion folly, son, but bound by oath, it shall be fulfilled.

label: i | go | iran
sentence: I go to Iran to plead your case to the Shah.”

label: sam | assemble | army
sentence: Sam assembled his army and set out to Iran with trumpets and cymbals heralding his arrival.

label: he | praise | god
sentence: When the messenger returned to Zal, he praised God and gave gifts to the poor and his servants.

label: soul | burn | longing
sentence: Yet no sleep touched Zal’s eyes, nor could he find rest; his soul burned with longing.

label: slave | come | letter
sentence: One day, a slave came with Sam’s letter for Rodabeh.

label: she | rejoice | ∅
sentence: She rejoiced and sent back a precious crown and ring.

label: she | meet | sindokht
sentence: On leaving her chamber, she met Queen Sindokht, who questioned her sharply about her comings and goings.

label: trembling | lie | ∅
sentence: The slave, trembling, lied that she sold jewels and robes.

label: sindokht | demand | proof
sentence: Sindokht demanded proof and found the tiara Rodabeh had embroidered in the slave’s sleeve.

label: sindokht | order | slave
sentence: Angered, Sindokht ordered the slave chained and called her daughter.

label: she | say | rodabeh
sentence: To Rodabeh she said, “O moon of noble race, blind to all but goodness, how have you strayed on evil paths?

label: man | mean | which
sentence: For which man are they meant?”

label: rodabeh | ashame | ∅
sentence: Rodabeh, ashamed, confessed all.

label: sindokht | fear | wrath
sentence: Sindokht feared the Shah’s wrath and wept alone in sorrow.

label: mihrab | enter | ∅
sentence: Mihrab entered and found her grieving.

label: she | tell | he
sentence: She told him of Rodabeh’s love for Zal.

label: heart | trouble | ∅
sentence: Mihrab’s heart troubled as well, knowing Kabul could not withstand the Shah’s might.

label: minuchihr | disturb | ∅
sentence: Minuchihr, too, was disturbed, fearing this union was Ahriman’s evil plot.

label: he | order | nauder
sentence: He ordered Nauder to summon Sam.

label: sam | come | shah
sentence: Sam came gladly before the Shah, kissing the ground and blessing him.

label: minuchihr | welcome | he
sentence: Minuchihr welcomed him, asked of the wars and the Mazinderan Deevs, and Sam told his battles.

label: shah | rejoice | valor
sentence: The Shah rejoiced at his valor, praised him, and called for a feast where heroes drank and feasted through the night.

label: court | open | dawn
sentence: At dawn, the king’s court opened for petitioners.

label: sam | come | ∅
sentence: Sam came first to speak of Zal’s love, but Minuchihr would not let him speak fully.

label: he | command | ∅
sentence: Instead, he commanded: “Go with your army.

label: ∅ | let | ∅
sentence: March on Kabul, burn Mihrab’s house, destroy the serpent’s seed; let none survive.

label: earth | rid | brood
sentence: The earth must be rid of this brood.”

label: sam | see | anger
sentence: Sam saw the Shah’s anger and knew words would not help.

label: he | bow | earth
sentence: He bowed on the earth and said, “I obey.”

label: army | advance | ∅
sentence: His army advanced, shaking the land.

label: news | bring | despair
sentence: News of Sam’s approach brought despair to Kabul.

label: house | fill | weeping
sentence: Mihrab’s house was filled with weeping.

label: zal | be | ∅
sentence: But Zal was angry and went to his father’s camp, asking for an audience.

label: sam | grant | it
sentence: Sam granted it.

label: zal | remind | sam
sentence: Zal reminded Sam of his oath, begging mercy for Kabul’s innocents.

label: heart | move | ∅
sentence: Sam’s heart moved.

label: you | speak | ∅
sentence: “You speak rightly, my son.

label: i | be | birth
sentence: I have been unjust to you since birth.

label: i | find | remedy
sentence: Hold your wrath; I will find a remedy.

label: you | bear | letter
sentence: You shall bear a letter to the Shah, and when he sees you, his heart will soften.”

label: zal | kiss | hand
sentence: Zal kissed his father’s hand and received blessings.

label: sam | write | letter
sentence: Sam wrote the letter recounting his deeds for Iran, his wars, his slaying of the dragon, and the peace he brought.

label: he | fight | ∅
sentence: Growing old, he could no longer fight, but his brave son followed him.

label: heart | consume | ∅
sentence: Yet his son’s heart was consumed by love—a longing that might kill him if denied.

label: sam | entrust | matter
sentence: Sam entrusted the matter to the Shah’s wisdom.

label: zal | set | letter
sentence: Zal set out with the letter, accompanied by the kingdom’s finest.

sentence: Fear gripped Kabul; Mihrab wondered how to calm the King.

sentence: He said to Sindokht, “The King is enraged because of you and Rodabeh.

sentence: Since I cannot face him, I will lead Rodabeh to his court and kill her before his eyes.

label: wrath | ease | ∅
sentence: Perhaps his wrath will ease thus.”

sentence: Sindokht, quick-witted, kept silent, then asked for the treasury key.

label: ∅ | let | ∅
sentence: “Let me take what I need and go to Sam.

label: i | sway | he
sentence: Perhaps I can sway him to spare Kabul.”

label: mihrab | agree | ∅
sentence: Mihrab, distraught, agreed.

label: sindokht | leave | ∅
sentence: Sindokht left, bringing with her immense gifts: three hundred thousand gold coins, sixty silver-trimmed horses with slaves bearing cups of musk and precious stones, two hundred camels, and four elephants from India laden with brocade and carpets.

label: retinue | stretch | mile
sentence: Her retinue stretched two miles beyond Kabul’s gates.

sentence: In Seistan, Sindokht asked Sam’s guards for entrance.

label: sam | welcome | she
sentence: Sam welcomed her, and she bowed, blessing him.

label: she | lay | treasure
sentence: She laid out the treasures, marveling at their worth.

sentence: Sam wondered why a woman bore gifts of such value from a land so rich.

label: he | say | ∅
sentence: Accepting the gifts, he said, “Let these be given to my son’s treasurer.”

label: ∅ | ask | sam
sentence: Sindokht, encouraged, asked Sam why he wished to destroy Kabul.

label: sam | demand | ∅
sentence: Sam demanded, “Are you Mihrab’s slave or wife?

label: she | be | daughter
sentence: Is she really your daughter whom Zal saw?

label: ∅ | tell | i
sentence: Tell me of her beauty that I may judge if she is worthy of my son.”

label: sindokht | reply | ∅
sentence: Sindokht replied, “Swear to me first that you will spare my life and those dear to me.”

label: sam | take | hand
sentence: Sam took her hand and swore a solemn oath.

sentence: Freed of fear, Sindokht revealed all: her lineage from Zohak, her marriage to Mihrab, and her daughter Rodabeh’s love for Zal.

label: she | ask | sam
sentence: She asked Sam to punish only the wicked in Kabul and spare the innocent, lest day turn to night.

sentence: Sam vowed, “By my life and oath, I shall protect you and your people.

label: zal | marry | rodabeh
sentence: Zal shall marry Rodabeh, though she be from a foreign race.”

label: he | plead | ∅
sentence: “Tell me of Rodabeh,” he pleaded.

sentence: Sindokht said, “If Zal wishes to gladden our hearts, he must come himself and see our moon with his own eyes.”

sentence: Sam smiled and replied, “Rest easy; all will end as you desire.”

sentence: Sindokht’s face shone with hope.

sentence: Zal gave the Shah Sam’s letter.

sentence: Reading it, the king was pained but promised to fulfill Zal’s wish.

sentence: The nobles and Shah feasted all night.

sentence: The Shah’s wise men studied the stars for three days, then declared joyfully: “The son of Sam and daughter of Mihrab shall be a glorious pair.

sentence: Their son will be a mighty warrior who will expand Iran’s glory, defeat evildoers, and bring prosperity.”

sentence: Minuchihr commanded secrecy.

sentence: He tested Zal’s wisdom with riddles asked by the wise men.

sentence: The Shah then tested Zal’s strength.

sentence: The nobles cheered; Minuchihr showered Zal with gifts.

sentence: The Shah replied to Sam’s letter: “O brave hero, I have seen your son and find him worthy.

sentence: I grant your wish and send him back to you satisfied.

sentence:
May no enemy harm him.”

After receiving the king’s leave, Zal returned proudly.

sentence: The army prepared to march; a messenger was sent to Mihrab telling him that Sam and Zal were coming to Kabul.

sentence: Sindokht shared the good news with Rodabeh, and they adorned the house as a paradise with golden carpets, ivory thrones, and scented floors.

sentence: After a month, Sam returned to Seistan with Zal and Rodabeh following.
